principles: Console health summaries and internal degradation alerts must consume the same observability contracts already defined for metrics, dashboards, health checks, and business metrics rather than inventing parallel status semantics.

Tenant- and workspace-scoped summaries must preserve strict multi-tenant isolation by exposing attributable degradation only and masking platform-internal implementation detail when that detail cannot be safely attributed.

Internal alerts are operational and auditable signals; they may drive console summaries and operator response, but they do not create a public notification or external delivery API commitment in this increment.
Freshness loss is itself a reportable health and alerting condition; stale evidence must remain explicit instead of being presented as current healthy state.

status_vocabulary.operational_meaning: All required evidence is fresh and no required component or downstream business-impact signal indicates degradation.
The platform or requested scope remains partially serving but at least one required signal indicates degraded behavior or elevated operational risk.
A required component or scope-critical dependency is unavailable and the requested summary scope should be treated as materially impaired.
The evidence backing the summary is older than the allowed freshness threshold and must not be treated as a current health statement.
The system cannot determine a safe current summary because evidence is incomplete, masked, or missing.

platform.rule: Platform summary is unavailable when any required health component is unavailable, degraded when none are unavailable but at least one required component is degraded or a severity high business-impact alert is active, stale when freshness is beyond threshold, unknown when evidence is incomplete, otherwise healthy.

tenant.rule: Tenant summary inherits platform_condition when a platform-scoped degradation affects tenant-visible capability, reports tenant_local when tenant-attributable evidence is degraded, becomes stale when tenant-scoped evidence exceeds freshness threshold, unknown when isolation-safe evidence is incomplete, otherwise healthy.

workspace.rule: Workspace summary inherits platform_condition or tenant_local before reporting workspace_local, becomes stale when workspace evidence exceeds freshness threshold, unknown when workspace-safe evidence is incomplete, otherwise healthy.

platform.rule: Platform summaries may name required components and platform-wide business-impact domains because the viewer already has platform-wide authority.

tenant.rule: Tenant summaries must not expose cross-tenant identifiers, raw infrastructure coordinates, or platform-internal dependency topology that cannot be safely attributed.

workspace.rule: Workspace summaries must preserve tenant_id and workspace_id context and may not widen to unrelated tenant/workspace evidence.

severity_levels.description: Informational condition useful for awareness but not an active incident by default.
Actionable condition that should be investigated before tenant-visible impact grows.
Material degradation with probable tenant-visible impact or elevated operational risk.
Severe degradation or outage requiring immediate operational response.

categories.description: Raised when a required health component changes between healthy, degraded, unavailable, stale, or unknown in a way that should update console summaries and operator awareness.
Raised when normalized workload or API error ratios remain above an operator-defined threshold long enough to represent meaningful degradation rather than a transient spike.
Raised when required probe or summary evidence exceeds the freshness threshold and the console must show stale instead of current health.
Raised when a bounded business metric deviates materially from expected posture and should inform console impact summaries or operational triage.

suppression_defaults.boundary_rule: Duplicate alerts for the same scope/category/evidence identity inside the suppression window collapse into a suppressed follow-up record.
suppression_defaults.repeat_summary_behavior: Repeat alerts increment counters and preserve the latest observed evidence without creating an unbounded stream of console-facing duplicates.

rules: Tenant and workspace alerts must use bounded degradation attribution labels instead of exposing raw platform-internal coordinates or cross-tenant detail.
Alert payloads may include stable component ids only when that component is explicitly safe and relevant for the requested summary scope.
Suppressed alerts remain queryable for audit and debugging but must preserve the same masking posture as active alerts.

downstream_consumers.uses: Consumes scope-safe summary cards and degradation attribution for platform, tenant, and workspace console views.
Consumes alert categories, severity levels, lifecycle semantics, and audience routing for internal operator alerting surfaces.
Consumes summary statuses, freshness semantics, and alert categories for T06 smoke verification coverage.
